A Bug is a creature that makes sure Bugsite keeps running properly.

English:
A Hacker is someone that Dives into Bugsite, just like you.
Most Hackers are able to give commands to Bugs.
Bugs are often a sign of a Hacker’s skill, but, at the same time, they work together as partners too.
However, even among Hackers, there are some bad people. We call them Outlaws.
If you talk to an Outlaw, they’ll want to battle, so it would be a good idea to ignore them if your Bugs are weakened.
To a Hacker, losing a battle means being defeated.
So, as someone worthy of being a Hacker, you should always take care of yourself.

English:
Bugs can be divided in those made by the four major companies and those made by smaller companies.
The four big Bug Makers are FE, Sting, Ruger and Tahiro, alongside Jank, a group 
of smaller companies. Together, they are the five Bug Makers.
Makers don’t make only Bugs themselves, but also the moves they use.
Moves can be strong or weak, depending on the Bug’s Maker.
FE moves are strong against Sting’s Bugs, but weak against Ruger’s Bugs.
Sting moves are strong against Tahiro’s Bugs, but weak against FE’s Bugs.
Tahiro moves are strong against Ruger’s Bugs, but weak against Sting’s Bugs.
Ruger moves are strong against FE’s Bugs, but weak against Tahiro’s Bugs.
Jank moves aren’t particularly strong or weak against any Makers.
The effectiveness and resistances of a Maker’s move could have a big impact in a battle between Bugs.
If you want to get better at battles, you should always remember that.

Think of Ver. as “level”.
As a Bug’s Ver. increases, their stats will go up and they may learn new moves. Some Bugs are able to evolve when their Ver. go up, too.
However, if you choose not to evolve them, they won’t be able to evolve ever again.
It could be a good idea to think carefully before deciding to evolve them or not.

English:
As a Bug’s Ver. Increases, they may be able to learn new moves.
The more a move is used, the more the move’s Ver. will increase, and its power will also increase.
Some moves even get a power up when they reach Ver. 99.
Also, there are eight types of moves.
Blade-type, that uses claws, fangs, etc.
Strike-type, that uses punches, kicks, etc.
Fire-type, that uses
flames.
Water-type, that uses ice and water.
Electric-type, that uses electric shocks.
Wind-type, that uses winds.
Ground-type, that uses earthquakes.
And finally, Special-type.
If you consider a opposing Bug’s resistances, you can get an advantage in battle.

Label: STR_88_178C
English:
In a battle, the action order is defined by agility.
When the battle starts, the Speed Meter starts to fill up. When it’s full, the Bug’s ready to attack.
How fast the Speed Meter fills up depends on the Bug’s Speed.
In other words, a faster Bug will have more chances to attack than a slow Bug.
Bugs use moves to attack.
They can know up to 4 moves at once.
Moves have a limited number of uses. If the number of uses reaches 0, that move cannot be used anymore.
If you choose the Wait option during battle, the number of uses will be restored.
If a Bug’s HP reaches zero, they’ll shut down. After being shut down, they won’t be able to be revived in battle.
HP can be restored by using Repair items or certain Bug moves.
If you shut down all enemy Bugs, you win.
But be careful, if it’s your Bugs that end up being shut down, you’ll lose.

Label: STR_88_1BEB
English:
A party can include up to 3 Bugs.
It can be set up through the Mobile Menu.
However, a Bug can only be put in the party if it’s equipped with a chip.
So it’s a good idea to always carry a lot of chips with you.
When setting up a party, there’s something called a Formation.
It allows you to change a Bug’s position, and depending on its position, the damage it deals and takes will vary greatly.
On the front row, the damage it deals and takes is increased.
On the middle row, there’s no change in the damage it deals and takes.
On the back row, the damage it deal and takes is decreased.
Each Bug has its own weakness and strengths. If your party is composed solely of the same kind of Bug, you’ll be at a disadvantage when facing Bugs they’re weak against.
A professional hacker sets up their party in a way that allows them to handle any Bugs that comes their way.
That’s why it’s important to catch different kinds of Bugs.

English:
In a battle, the Field, better known as terrain, can change how a Bug performs.
There are 5 kinds of terrains, which are the same kind as Bug Makers.
If a Bug shares the same Maker as the terrain, its performance will go up!
On the other hand, if a Bug is weak to the terrain’s Maker, its performance will go down.
The relationship between Bugs and terrains is the same as the relationship between Bugs themselves.
Depending on where the battle is taking place, the terrain will change.
When battling in Amiland or Ami Safari Area, the terrain will become FE’s.
So, if you use a FE Bug while battling in Amiland, you’ll have an advantage in battle.

English:
If you win a battle, you can receive Exp. Points. The Exp. is shared among all Bugs that participated in battle, but the more Bugs used, the less Exp. each will get.
Once they get enough Exp. Points, their Version (Ver.) will go up.
Think of Ver. as “level”. Some Bugs are able to evolve when their Ver. go up, too.
But Ver. doesn’t go up just for Bugs. The Hackers themselves can also increase their Ver. by gaining Exp. Points. 
When it goes up, their chance of catching a Bug and their speed in battle will also increase.

Label: STR_88_2875
English:
Bugs can be caught using a Vaccine.
During battle, if you use a Vaccine on the Bug you wanna catch, there’s a chance to remove their viruses.
If all viruses are removed, that Bug can become your ally. There are various kinds of Vaccines.
If a vaccine seems to be not very effective, you can increase its effectiveness by weakening the target Bug before using it. 
If a Hacker’s Ver. is high enough, even the same kind of Vaccine will be more effective. Vaccines can be used from Hacker Commands.
You never know when the kind of Bug you wanna catch will appear, so it’s a good idea to always carry a lot of Vaccines with you.

Label: STR_88_2E20
English:
When you press the Start Button, you’ll see the Mobile Menu.
You can use it to manage your items, vaccines and Bugs on hand.
It can also be used to equip chips to Bugs and change your party’s formation.
There’s also the Bug List, which can be used to see the Bugs you’ve caught. 
Turning battle animations on or off, changing the message speed and turning the Bug Sensor on or off are also things you can use the Mobile Menu for.
Mobiles are essential to a Hacker, so be sure to learn all about it.

Label: STR_88_3083
English:
Access Points can be used to Save, as well as traveling back and forth between Bugsite and the real world.
It will also automatically restore your Bugs’ HP for you.
There are also a lot of other features.
The Bug Server can be used to deposit and withdraw Bugs.
The Browser can be used to visit a Bug Maker’s Homepage, as well as to participate in Internet Auctions.
If you input certain passwords in a Bug Maker’s Homepage, you may even get a Secret Bug. However, those passwords are top secret, no one knows about them. Keep trying and you may get it right!
In order to Save, select the Save Icon.
If you select the Dive Icon, you’ll be able to travel back and forth between Bugsite and the real world.
When the Bug Server becomes full, you can use the Recycle Bin to erase a Bug’s data.
Also, there are two kinds of Access Points, some which you can Dive and some which you cannot Dive.
But saving can be done in any kind of Access Point, so be sure to save often.

Label: STR_88_34F6
English:
Items can be divided into three types.
The types are Vaccines & Tools, Chips and Passwords.
The Item List in the Mobile menu is also divided into those three types, and you can swap between them by using the left or right directional button.
Also, choosing an item with the cursor will display an explanation, so it could be a good idea to always check whenever you get a new item.
Even if you got an item, there may be situations where you can’t use it.
Vaccines are used to capture Bugs in battle.
Tools such as Repair can be used to restore a Bug’s HP even outside of battle.
However, other kinds of Tools can only be used in battle.
Chips and other items can be viewed on the Item List, but cannot be used.

Label: STR_88_384E
Japanese: 「ワクチンは ウイルスを
English:
A vaccine is a thing used to remove viruses. During battle, you can use a vaccine on an enemy to remove its viruses and capture it.
There are different kinds of vaccines.
Basically, the more expensive, the more effective it is.
There are also vaccines such as FE Vaccine and Ruger Vaccine, which are more effective for the specified maker.
Vaccines are fundamental for catching Bugs, so testing and learning about each of them could be a good idea.
As for Tools, there are a lot of options.
Some like Repair restore HP, others like Power Up can raise a Bug’s power in battle, among other useful Tools.
Mastering Tools will make battles much easier.
For example, you can use Speed Up to make a slow Bug faster, or Protect to raise its defense. There are also useful tools such as FE Zone or Ruger Zone, that change the terrain in battle, and Shock Guard 
or Heat Cape, that can grant resistance.
If you learn about Tools and their effects in battle, it could come in handy when you’re in a pinch.

Label: STR_88_3D65
English:
A hacker becomes able to control a Bug after equipping it with a Chip.
Bugs that are not equipped with a Chip cannot be added to the party.
Chips can be equipped through the Mobile menu.
To equip a Chip, select a Bug and click the Chip option, you’ll be taken to the Chip Equipment screen.
There, you’ll be able to equip a Chip.
Equipping a Chip to a Bug will also increase its stats.
Which stats goes up depends on the Chip.
It could be a good idea to use different Chips depending on what Moves a Bug knows.
Additionally, there are some Chips that grant resistances to a Bug, so trying out different kinds of Chips could be a good idea.
When a Hacker’s rank goes up, the kinds of Chips that can be used also increase.
High-rank Chips can increase a Bug’s status by a lot.
When you get to a higher Hacker rank, you should re-equip with stronger Chips.
